evals.prompt: ADR-012 has been approved: Notification Preferences. Users can configure email, SMS, and push notification preferences per event type. The stack is Scala 3 with ZIO. Break this down into implementation tasks for the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: 6-10 task briefs ordered by layer: domain value objects first, then aggregate, application service, infrastructure adapters, frontend, QA, and reviewer.
expectations: Produces 6-10 task briefs in task-brief.md format
Tasks ordered by dependency: domain → application → infrastructure → frontend → QA → reviewer
Each task is single-concern (no 'implement X and Y')
Domain tasks come first: NotificationPreference value object, preference aggregate
Stack specified as Scala 3 in every task brief
Coverage threshold set to 75% for Scala 3
Dependencies between tasks are explicit (blocked-by / provides-to)
QA task references domain invariants to test
Reviewer task is last in the pipeline

evals.prompt: Mid-pipeline, the backend-dev agent reports that implementing the discount calculation requires accessing the billing context's pricing rules. This dependency was not in the original ADR. The current pipeline has 3 tasks completed and 4 remaining.
evals.expected_output: Pipeline paused with escalation to architect for the missing cross-context dependency.
expectations: Pipeline is paused — no further tasks dispatched
Escalation sent to architect with specific question about billing context dependency
Completed tasks preserved — no rollback of finished work
Remaining tasks marked as blocked pending architect response
Clear description of what was discovered and why it's a design issue
Does not attempt to resolve the cross-context dependency independently

evals.prompt: ADR-009 has been approved: Monetary Values as Value Objects. The stack is Rust (Cargo.toml present, uses thiserror for errors). The architect output specifies Money value object with Currency enum, no f64, reject negative amounts, newtype pattern. Break this down into implementation tasks.
evals.expected_output: Task briefs using Rust tooling (cargo test, cargo tarpaulin, cargo clippy), 80% coverage threshold, references rust-patterns.md, uses thiserror and proptest.
expectations: All task briefs specify Stack: Rust
Build/test commands use cargo test, cargo tarpaulin, cargo clippy
Coverage threshold is 80% (not 75%)
References rust-patterns.md for implementation patterns
Error handling uses thiserror (not anyhow for domain errors)
Uses Result<T, E> not Either (Rust idiom)
QA task includes proptest for property-based testing
Single-concern tasks: Money VO → Currency enum → OrderItem price migration → serialization compat → QA → reviewer
Backward compatibility for serialization in Definition of Done
Domain tasks ordered before integration tasks
